codeReview.summary: The refactor largely implements the intended decoupling (hook/metadata-driven node creation, socket propagation, route display, and generic node rendering), but the submitted commit is not shippable because it introduced blocking TypeScript compile errors in core flow UI files.

issues.description: `memo` is used for `GradientEdge` but not imported from React in this commit (`import` line only includes useCallback/useEffect/useMemo/useRef/useState), causing TS2304 and breaking typecheck.

`resolveNodeRendererComponent` accepts `NodeDefinition | null` (line 35), but it is called with `getNodeDefinition(...)` which returns `NodeDefinition | undefined`, producing TS2345 nullability/type mismatch.

`buildRouteDisplayState` expects `definition: NodeDefinition | null` (line 26), but the caller passes `definition` derived from `getNodeDefinition(...)` (line 109), which can be `undefined`; this introduces TS2322/TS2345 nullability errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker procedure does not require a TypeScript compile check before handoff, which allowed this feature to ship with TS errors that were later caught by scrutiny.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:45-48` requires `bun run test --run` and `bun run lint` but not `bunx tsc --noEmit`; a follow-up feature was created specifically to fix TS errors introduced by this feature (`mission features.json:636-650`, fix-typescript-errors-plugin-api).

summary: FAIL — commit fb94f714eaab3e11b1ec75b18b5643e5fba258dc delivered the intended frontend decoupling direction, but introduced blocking TypeScript errors in canvas/node/properties-panel, so the feature did not fully satisfy quality gates at submission time.
